title: Roku Q4 2023 Earnings Summary
text: Roku exceeded Wall Street expectations in Q4 2023, reporting revenue of $984.4 million (up 14% year-over-year) against analyst forecasts of $968.2 million. The company posted a net loss of $78.3 million, or 55 cents per share, compared to a $237.1 million loss in the prior year. The streaming platform surpassed 80 million active accounts globally by year-end, adding 10 million accounts throughout 2023. Users streamed 106 billion hours in total, representing a 21% increase from the previous year. Platform revenue reached $828.9 million, growing 13%, driven by "strong contributions from streaming services distribution activities and video advertising." However, this gain was partially offset by declining media and entertainment promotional spending. Despite beating expectations, Roku's stock fell more than 14% in after-hours trading due to the company's cautionary outlook. Management described "challenging" conditions ahead for the media and entertainment sector throughout 2024, citing an "uneven ad market recovery." Operating expenses declined 12% to $542 million, reflecting prior layoffs of approximately 500 employees and office consolidation efforts. Free cash flow improved significantly to $175.9 million in 2023 from negative $154.1 million the prior year.
